page: Amazon Web Services i4g.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The server includes 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core testing reaching 97,040.58 and software compilation with Clang scoring 43,086.0. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is highly efficient at 3,784.1 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds remain average. Single-core performance is a relative tradeoff, with weaker scores in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests.

This hardware profile is highly suited for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and caching layers, as demonstrated by strong Redis SET performance of 72.23 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 45.46 GB/sec. The integration of massive local NVMe storage alongside a high-density ARM architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency for I/O-intensive workloads and large-scale data stores, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores via the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 38 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional Redis SET throughput of 72.23 million operations per second and static web serving speeds of 45.46 GB/sec. However, single-core processing and LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs. This server is highly efficient for large-scale database operations, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving workloads requiring dense local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 ARMv8 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 64 ARMv8 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized instance delivering massive local NVMe capacity and strong multi-core performance for intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
